question: Will CPI rise more than 0.4% in April 2026?
reasoning: March 2026 CPI data (releasing April 10) is expected to show a dramatic spike to 0.9-1.0% monthly increase due to Iran war energy shock. With gasoline prices up 19% in two weeks and energy expected to jump 10.6% monthly, March will create difficult year-over-year comparisons for April. Even assuming energy stabilizes, the elevated base effects from March's spike make a >0.4% April increase highly probable as secondary inflation effects filter through to other categories.
key_factors: Iran war causing energy price shock - gasoline up 57 cents/gallon (19%) in two weeks
March CPI consensus forecasts 0.9-1.0% monthly increase, creating high base effects
Energy expected to contribute 10.6% monthly increase in March CPI report
Secondary inflation effects from transportation and fertilizer costs building
Fed revised 2026 inflation forecast upward from 2.4% to 2.7%
risks: Rapid de-escalation of Iran conflict could stabilize energy prices faster than expected
Strong dollar could offset some commodity price increases
Shelter inflation continuing to moderate could provide offset
Used vehicle prices showing weakness could dampen core inflation
